remediations.description: Relaxed DKIM/SPF alignment combined with a weak SPF policy allows cousin-domain spoofing.
remediations.dns_fix: Set adkim=s; aspf=s in DMARC record for strict alignment.
remediations.bind_command: Update adkim and aspf tags to 's' in existing DMARC TXT record.
remediations.ps_command: Update adkim and aspf tags to 's' in existing DMARC TXT record.

remediations.description: SPF ~all (softfail) tags unauthorized mail but does not reject it. Upgrade to -all when ready.
remediations.bind_command: Change ~all to -all in existing SPF TXT record.
remediations.ps_command: Change ~all to -all in existing SPF TXT record.

remediations.description: No DKIM keys discovered across 15 common selectors. DKIM signing may not be configured.
remediations.dns_fix: Generate a DKIM keypair and publish the public key at <selector>._domainkey.<domain>.

spoof_results.detail: SPF ~all tags unauthorized senders but does not reject. The message will likely land in spam, not be dropped.
No DKIM keys were discovered. The receiving MTA cannot verify a DKIM signature, so this check is effectively skipped for spoofed mail.
DMARC p=reject at pct=100. If both SPF and DKIM fail alignment, the message will be rejected outright.

spoof_results.verdict: Spoofed email would be REJECTED by a compliant MTA.
spoof_results.detail: This is a prediction based on published DNS records. Actual delivery depends on the receiving MTA's implementation and local overrides.
